model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 1","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimizer setup is already strong, so the next gain should come from a better MLP shape. A deeper, uniformly wide 3-layer stack should improve capacity and optimization geometry without adding much complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The optimizer and schedule are already in a good regime; the remaining lever is the peak step size. Dropping AdamW to 3e-3 is a conservative adjustment that should tighten convergence without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"layers.append(nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim))","old":"layers.append(nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run now has a strong optimizer and schedule, so the next low-risk gain is normalization. LayerNorm is often a better fit for flattened MLPs than BatchNorm while keeping the rest of the training recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"layers.append(nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim))","old":"layers.append(nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 1","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"LayerNorm was a regression, so revert to the better normalization and retune the optimization scale. A smaller batch with a shorter warmup and a slightly lower peak LR should restore stability while improving the stochasticity of the large-step run.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The smaller-batch/shorter-warmup variant regressed, so revert to the stronger large-batch training regime and spend the next increment on capacity. A modest width increase is the lowest-complexity way to test whether the MLP is still underpowered.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer and schedule are already in a good regime, so the next low-complexity test is more capacity at the same width. Adding one more 320-wide hidden block is the safest way to probe whether the MLP is still underparameterized.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The fourth layer regressed, so return to the better 3-layer form and spend the capacity budget on width instead. The earlier trend suggests this MLP still benefits more from a wider hidden stack than from extra depth.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The wider 3-layer MLP is now the better architecture, so the remaining lever is a smaller peak step size. Backing off slightly from 3e-3 should reduce overshoot without changing the rest of the working recipe.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The lower learning rate regressed, so restore the stronger 3e-3 peak step size and make AdamW adapt a bit faster with beta2=0.99. This keeps the width and schedule that were already working while testing a more responsive optimizer state.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 1","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The optimizer settings are already close to good, so the remaining easy win is to spend less of the fixed step budget warming up. Cutting warmup to one epoch should give the model more time at its effective learning rate before cosine decay takes over.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 26","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Warmup of one epoch was too short, so restore the more stable two-epoch schedule and spend the next increment on width. The current 3-layer MLP still appears to benefit from additional hidden capacity more than from further optimizer tweaks.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 26","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run is still improving as width increases, while the optimizer recipe is already tuned. A small further width bump is the cleanest next test of whether the flat MLP is still capacity-limited.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current width seems near the useful regime, so the next gain is more likely to come from regularization than from more capacity. A slightly stronger AdamW decay is a low-cost way to test whether the model is beginning to overfit.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 26","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.25e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The wider model and stronger decay did not help, so revert to the better capacity/regularization point and probe a slightly higher peak learning rate. This keeps the successful beta and warmup setup intact while testing for a bit more optimization headroom.","secondary_modes":["layout","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.25e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current configuration is stable and still improved when the peak learning rate was nudged up, so there is likely a little more headroom. Increasing it slightly again is the lowest-complexity next test.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.75e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last increase still helped, which suggests the current setup can tolerate a little more peak learning rate. Nudge it once more and keep the rest of the tuned recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.75e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.995)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last rate bump was too aggressive, so back off to the better 3.5e-3 peak and smooth the optimizer a bit more with beta2=0.995. The architecture is already settled, so this is the cleanest remaining optimization-only test.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.995)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The only regression from the current best setting is the beta2 change, so restore the previously better AdamW moment smoothing. The rest of the recipe is already at the strongest visible point.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
